reasoning: The business is exceptional and the bull case is real. But a 685% gain on a position that's 42% of your portfolio is not investing — it's luck combined with lack of risk management. You should have been trimming this position months ago as it grew from a reasonable 3-5% to 10% to 20% to 42%. At minimum, reduce by 50% now (sell ~37 shares, leaving ~37 shares). This brings NXSN to ~21% of portfolio — still overweight, but no longer dangerous. Use proceeds to either reduce other positions, build cash, or buy something uncorrelated. The stock can continue tocompound and you still have exposure — but your downside risk if Q1 earnings disappoints is now genuinely portfolio-destructive. ENTRY/EXIT CONDITIONS: Reduce 50% now at current price (₪31,430). After trim, hold remaining position with stop
entryConditions: Add only on meaningful pullback to ₪22,000-24,000 range with fundamentals clearly intact
The ₪20,000-22,000 "add" level in the old strategy was dangerously passive for a position of this size.
exitConditions: After trim, hold remaining position with stop-loss at ₪24,000 (~23% below current, close to the 52-week median)

catalysts.description: earnings miss could trigger a 20-30% drawdown

bullCase: NextVision is a genuine Israeli defense tech compounder — 46% revenue growth, 62% net margins, zero debt, $288M backlog, and a 2026 revenue target of $275M implying +64% growth. The Iran-Israel conflict is a structural multi-year tailwind for drone-based ISR demand. Chairman called it "a train that has already left the station." The stock pulled back 23% from ATH — healthy consolidation in an otherwise extraordinary uptrend. P/E at 88x is extreme but arguably justified given the growth trajectory if 2026 targets are met.
bearCase: P/E at 88x is a fortress of a valuation — priced for not just meeting but exceeding $275M guidance. A single production stumble or Q1 earnings miss could trigger a 20-30% drawdown. EU revenue concentration (56%) is a geopolitical risk. Most critically: this position represents ~42% of your entire portfolio. A 23% pullback from ATH on a 42%-weighted position wiped out gains on a significant portion of your net worth in weeks. Concentration of this magnitude is the real risk, not the stock's fundamentals.
